steps.rationale: Switch to a more robust ASR path and a SOAP-style stronger note model to recover missed clinical details and produce cleaner chart structure on choppy audio.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact preserves many core history and exam facts and maintains a basic clinical-note structure, but it falls short of the expected standard. Its main problems are missing key clinical details such as allergy and full vitals, a generic and incomplete plan without explicit return precautions, and obvious meta/evaluation leakage that makes the note feel less professional and less chart-ready.

changes.before: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.
changes.after: Whisper plus SOAP note generation with balanced cleanup for fragmented recordings.
changes.summary: description changed from `Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.` to `Whisper plus SOAP note generation with balanced cleanup for fragmented recordings.`

params -> pipeline config -> note generation -> prompt style changed from `brief` to `soap`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> denoise changed from `off` to `mild`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> silence trimming changed from `aggressive` to `moderate`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> vad changed from `strict` to `balanced`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> voice enhancement changed from `off` to `on`

steps.rationale: Test whether better transcription alone carries most of the gain by changing ASR and preprocessing while keeping generation relatively lightweight and brief.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact captures the high-level impression of a viral upper respiratory infection and uses a recognizable SOAP structure, but it underperforms on core clinical fidelity and chart readiness. Major omissions include the exact symptom timeline, worse-at-night history, dextromethorphan, penicillin allergy, most vitals, key lung and throat exam findings, and the explicit return precautions and follow-up window. The note is further weakened by transcript/leakage artifacts and meta commentary, making it less complete, less professional, and less actionable than the expected note.

steps.rationale: Keep the current ASR family but reduce over-aggressive preprocessing and ask for a more detailed note to improve completeness without changing too many components at once.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact preserves only part of the clinical picture and falls well short of the expected note. Its main weaknesses are substantial factual omissions, a weak and nonspecific plan with missing return precautions, and a nonclinical presentation style that includes transcript leakage and editorial commentary instead of polished documentation.

changes.after: Parakeet with gentler preprocessing and detailed note prompting for better completeness.
